page: Microsoft Azure GS4-4 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the GS family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon E5-2698B v3 processor with 4 dedicated physical cores and 4 vCPUs operating at a base clock speed of 2.0 GHz. The x86_64 architecture includes a 40 MB L3 cache and supports AVX and AES instructions. It is equipped with 224.0 GB of system memory, yielding an exceptionally high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 56.0 GB per core. Local storage is provided via a bundled 459 GB drive, while no GPU accelerators are present.

Benchmark results indicate that the GS4-4 exhibits limited computational throughput. Both single-core and multi-core CPU performance in stress-ng tests fall into the bottom 10% tier. Similarly, Geekbench single-core and multi-core scores are categorized as weak, placing in the bottom 25%. Memory performance is mixed; while the Passmark memory score achieves an average rating, the uncached large-block memory write bandwidth is poor, landing in the bottom 10%. Cryptographic, compression, and database operations consistently rank in the bottom 25% of tested systems.

From a resource allocation perspective, the GS4-4 offers high memory density relative to its low virtual core count. This configuration is qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads requiring massive memory footprints without demanding high CPU compute cycles. However, the tradeoff is significant: the older Intel Xeon architecture and low clock speed result in poor processing efficiency and low throughput for CPU-bound tasks, making it less suitable for compute-heavy applications.

The Microsoft Azure GS4-4 is best suited for memory-intensive workloads that do not require high CPU performance. Appropriate use cases include large in-memory caches, memory-bound database operations, and legacy enterprise applications requiring substantial RAM allocation but minimal processing power. It is not recommended for high-performance computing, intensive LLM inference, or CPU-bound compilation tasks.
description: The Microsoft Azure GS4-4 is a memory-optimized virtual machine powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2698B v3 processor with 4 dedicated vCPUs and 4 physical cores running at 2.0 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, it features an exceptionally high memory capacity of 224.0 GB (56.0 GB per core) and includes 459 GB of local storage. Benchmark data shows weak to poor CPU performance, with stress-ng and Geekbench scores falling into the bottom 10% and 25% respectively, though memory performance remains average. This server is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-heavy, compute-light workloads. It is best suited for in-memory caching, memory-bound databases, and legacy enterprise applications requiring vast RAM arrays without intensive processing demands.
og_description: Microsoft Azure GS4-4 is a memory-optimized VM featuring 4 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 224 GB of RAM, and 459 GB of local storage, designed for memory-intensive workloads with low CPU demands.

tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server featuring an exceptional memory-to-core ratio and local storage for memory-bound, low-compute enterprise workloads.

Massive 224.0 GB of system memory providing 56.0 GB per core
Bundled 459 GB of local storage with no GPU accelerators
Average memory performance paired with weak to poor CPU benchmark results
Best suited for in-memory caching and memory-bound database workloads
